description: This bar chart (Fig. 15) compares the model prediction differences between the baseline Extra Trees (ET) ensemble and the Genetic Algorithm-optimized Extra Trees (GA-Extra Trees) for yield strength prediction in Fe-0.25C-3Mn-2Al-0.4Si medium-manganese low-density steel. The x-axis denotes three intercritical annealing temperatures—750°C, 800°C, and 850°C—while the y-axis reports the prediction error in MPa. Red-gradient bars correspond to the unoptimized Extra Trees model, exhibiting errors of 34 MPa, 31 MPa, and 36 MPa, respectively. Blue-gradient bars represent the GA-Extra Trees model, showing markedly lower errors of 12 MPa, 10 MPa, and 19 MPa under identical processing conditions. Across all temperatures, GA hyperparameter optimization consistently reduces prediction deviation, with the largest absolute improvement at 750°C (a 22 MPa reduction). The figure demonstrates that integrating genetic algorithm optimization with the Extra Trees regressor substantially enhances predictive accuracy for multi-mechanism yield strength modeling in this alloy system.
